rationale: The citing paper uses Khan and Dhar (2006) in its core conceptual setup, not just as a peripheral mention. It cites this work when defining licensing as a post-success shift toward short-term goals and again as evidence that subgoal success can elevate self-concept and promote licensing. Those uses help frame the paper's central persistence-versus-licensing problem and motivate why measuring responses to subgoal success is important. The citation is therefore theoretically functional in framing and interpretation.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Khan and Dhar (2006) as a key foundation for the licensing side of its framework, including the self-concept-based route to indulgent choice after prior goal-consistent behavior. It then builds on that foundation by introducing the PLRM to explain which consumers persist versus license after subgoal success.
